Diagnostic Criteria: Syncope with chest pain/palpitations/exertion/SOB/neurologic deficits/GI bleed
Red Flag Criteria: Chest pain palpitations exertional syncope SOB neuro deficits GI bleed anticoagulants
Secondary Questions: Prodrome exertion palpitations GI bleed symptoms
Logic Notes: Default to ED for true syncope

Diagnostic Criteria: Clear trigger + prodrome + quick recovery and no red flags
Disposition Rationale: Needs evaluation to rule out high-risk causes
Dx_Key: Even likely vasovagal should be evaluated if true syncope
